chunks.text: Kristofer Pister is a Professor in EECS at UC Berkeley. Contact information: 512 Cory Hall, ksjp@berkeley.edu. Office hours: W 3:30-4:30; F 2:30-3:30, 512 Cory. Links include Personal Homepage, Publications, Dissertations, Research Support, and Thomas Parsons (tparsons@berkeley.edu).
chunks.question: What is Kristofer Pister's position and contact details?

chunks.text: Biography: He received a B.A. in Applied Physics from UC San Diego (1986), and M.S. and Ph.D. in EECS from UC Berkeley (1989, 1992). Before joining EECS faculty in 1996, he taught at UCLA Electrical Engineering. He developed Smart Dust, a cubic millimeter sensing/communication platform, earning the Alexander Schwarzkopf Prize in 2006. He also works on synthetic insects, described as 'Smart Dust with legs,' and received the Alfred F. Sperry Founder Award in 2009. Kris is co-Director of Berkeley Sensor and Actuator Center (BSAC) and Ubiquitous Swarm Lab.
chunks.question: What are Kristofer Pister's key achievements and roles?
chunks.answer: Developed Smart Dust and co-directs BSAC.

chunks.text: Education: Ph.D. in EECS from UC Berkeley (1992), M.S. in EECS from UC Berkeley (1989), B.A. in Applied Physics from UC San Diego (1986). Research areas: Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems (MEMS), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR) including Micro-robotics, Integrated Circuits (INC) including Low-power circuits. Research centers: Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center (BSAC), CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR), Robotics and Intelligent Machines Laboratory.
chunks.question: What are Kristofer Pister's education and research focuses?
chunks.answer: Ph.D. from UC Berkeley, focuses on MEMS and robotics.

chunks.text: Teaching Schedule: EE 194 IC Design Project: 16nm SoC for IoT (TuTh 11:00-12:29, Cory 540AB), EE 197-30 Field Study. Selected Publications include works on wireless inertial sensors, low-power transceivers, CMOS imaging receivers, ultra-low-energy ADCs, wireless optical communication, solar-powered robots, autonomous sensor nodes, MEMS design, smart pixels, energy considerations for Smart Dust, and microfabricated hinges, spanning from 1992 to 2007.
chunks.question: What does Kristofer Pister teach and research?
chunks.answer: Teaches IC design and publishes on sensors.

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: SSCS Innovative Education Award (2024), Bakar Fellows Spark Award (2020), Electrical Engineering Award for Outstanding Teaching (2018), Outstanding Advising Administrator (2017), ISA Albert F. Sperry Founder Award (2009), Ten People to Watch (2006), Alexander Schwarzkopf Prize (2006), NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (1996).
chunks.question: What awards has Kristofer Pister received?
chunks.answer: Multiple awards including Sperry and Schwarzkopf prizes.
